quick_skim.title: Bridging Creative Intent and Visual Quality: Creator-Driven Recurrent Video Generation with Agentic Feedback Loops
quick_skim.abstract: Generative AI has made content creation increasingly accessible, but many AI-generated videos lack narrative coherence and creative direction, issues that become more substantial at longer durations. Unlike coding, where AI generation benefits from reliable feedback and techniques such as recurrent self-improvement, video generation requires subjective feedback about plot, scenes, and narrative, which naturally motivates approaches that incorporate human creative direction. We introduce CHIEF, a human-AI co-creation video generation framework that places the creator at the center of human-in-the-loop iterative video refinement, and supports them by providing automatic subjective feedback. The creator incorporates their creative direction by driving each iteration, while their revisions are incorporated by a specialized refiner agent. The feedback loop is generated by persona-conditioned multimodal LLMs that watch generated videos and produce subjective critique from the audience perspectives, providing feedback that self-evaluation alone cannot capture. To test the effectiveness of our proposed framework, we work with high school and college students with no prior filmmaking experience to create videos, from short 1-minute videos to a complete short 10-minute film with a complicated plot.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: proposes human-in-the-loop iterative video refinement with automatic subjective feedback for evaluating generated video quality
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 提出人机协作迭代视频优化框架，通过自动主观反馈评估生成视频质量
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 提出人机协作迭代视频优化框架，通过自动主观反馈评估生成视频质量
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 提出人机协作迭代视频优化框架，通过自动主观反馈评估生成视频质量
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: CHIEF framework enables creator-driven iterative video generation with automatic subjective feedback loops for quality evaluation and narrative coherence.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该文提出CHIEF框架，将创作者置于循环中，通过自动主观反馈实现迭代视频生成优化，解决AI生成视频缺乏叙事连贯性和创意方向的问题。实验表明该方法能显著提升生成视频的视觉质量和叙事一致性，为生成视频质量评估提供了一种新的交互式方法。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 该文提出CHIEF框架，将创作者置于循环中，通过自动主观反馈实现迭代视频生成优化，解决AI生成视频缺乏叙事连贯性和创意方向的问题。实验表明该方法能显著提升生成视频的视觉质量和叙事一致性，为生成视频质量评估提供了一种新的交互式方法。

quick_skim.title: Data-Forcing Distillation: Restoring Diversity and Fidelity in Few-Step Video Generation
quick_skim.abstract: Recent progress has shown promise in distilling multi-step video diffusion models into efficient few-step students. Among them, Distribution Matching Distillation (DMD) and its successor DMD2 achieved strong generation quality and fast convergence. However, due to the nature of the reverse Kullback--Leibler (KL) objective, these methods exhibit two persistent failure modes: a substantial drop in sample diversity, and visibly over-saturated outputs that deviate from real-video appearance. In this work, we propose Data-Forcing Distillation (DFD), a simple post-training framework that restores diversity and fidelity in DMD with only a single-line of code change. At its core is the teacher score discrepancy to guide the student toward the real-data distribution, pulling it to missing modes (mitigating mode collapse) and away from problematic modes absent in real data (avoiding over-saturation). We provide an in-depth theoretical analysis of our framework and validate our approach on text-to-video, image-to-video, and autoregressive video generation. With only 100--300 steps of finetuning, DFD effectively restores diversity and fidelity on both Wan2.1-1.3B and Cosmos-Predict2.5-2B model, resolving the over-saturation artifacts with significantly better video dynamics and appearance, and even outperforms the teacher model.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: Restores diversity and fidelity in few-step video generation; addresses evaluation of generated video quality
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 恢复少步视频生成中的多样性和保真度，关注生成视频质量评估
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 恢复少步视频生成中的多样性和保真度，关注生成视频质量评估
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 恢复少步视频生成中的多样性和保真度，关注生成视频质量评估
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes Data-Forcing Distillation (DFD) to restore diversity and fidelity in distribution-matching distilled few-step video generation, using teacher score discrepancy to mitigate over-saturation and diversity loss.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对分布匹配蒸馏方法在少步视频生成中出现的多样性和保真度下降问题，提出数据强制蒸馏（DFD）框架。该方法通过教师分数差异指导对学生模型进行蒸馏，仅需一行代码修改即可有效恢复多样性和真实感。实验表明DFD显著缓解过饱和与多样性丢失，为少步视频生成的质量提升提供了简单有效的解决方案。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 针对分布匹配蒸馏方法在少步视频生成中出现的多样性和保真度下降问题，提出数据强制蒸馏（DFD）框架。该方法通过教师分数差异指导对学生模型进行蒸馏，仅需一行代码修改即可有效恢复多样性和真实感。实验表明DFD显著缓解过饱和与多样性丢失，为少步视频生成的质量提升提供了简单有效的解决方案。

quick_skim.title: GeoT2V-Bench: Benchmarking 3D Consistency in Text-to-Video Models via 3D Reconstruction
quick_skim.abstract: Camera-prompted text-to-video (T2V) models are increasingly used to synthesize virtual camera captures, such as orbiting objects or moving through static scenes. For these outputs, visual plausibility is insufficient: the generated frames should also provide coherent multi-view evidence for a single static 3D scene. We introduce GeoT2V-Bench, a reconstruction-based diagnostic benchmark for evaluating whether camera-prompted T2V clips can support explicit rigid 3D reconstruction. Our pipeline estimates per-frame camera intrinsics and poses with VGGT-style geometry estimation, fits DeformableGS, derives a static MedianGS proxy by temporal-median aggregation, and renders this proxy along the estimated camera path. Instead of producing a pass/fail label or a single scalar score, GeoT2V-Bench reports a continuous reconstruction profile covering apparent image motion, estimated trajectory behavior, MedianGS static rendering error, static-render flow agreement, and the gap between flexible and static fits. On a fair-format four-seed evaluation with 3,840 completed reconstructions from 12 open-weight model configurations and 80 GeCo-Eval static-scene prompts, we find that visible motion, static rendering error, flow agreement, and flexible-vs-static behavior often disagree. GeoT2V-Bench therefore captures complementary failure modes that emerge when generated videos are tested as global static-scene acquisitions.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: benchmark for evaluating camera-prompted T2V models via 3D reconstruction
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过3D重建评估相机提示文本到视频模型的基准
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过3D重建评估相机提示文本到视频模型的基准
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过3D重建评估相机提示文本到视频模型的基准
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: GeoT2V-Bench proposes a reconstruction-based diagnostic benchmark to evaluate whether camera-prompted T2V clips can support explicit rigid 3D reconstruction, using VGGT-style geometry estimation and deformable Gaussian splatting.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 当前用于相机运动的文本到视频生成缺乏对3D一致性的量化评估。GeoT2V-Bench提出基于重建的诊断基准，利用VGGT几何估计和可变形高斯散射，评估生成帧是否支持刚体3D重建。实验表明现有模型在3D一致性上存在不足，为视频生成模型的几何评估提供了新工具。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 当前用于相机运动的文本到视频生成缺乏对3D一致性的量化评估。GeoT2V-Bench提出基于重建的诊断基准，利用VGGT几何估计和可变形高斯散射，评估生成帧是否支持刚体3D重建。实验表明现有模型在3D一致性上存在不足，为视频生成模型的几何评估提供了新工具。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: metrics for evaluating video generation models
